title: Modular apparel
abstract: The present disclosure relates to modular garments comprised of a base piece of clothing that may be worn by itself as a standalone garment, which is attached to one or more component pieces to create an entirely new garment such as a dress, jumpsuit, pantsuit, romper, singlet, etc. To further modify the look of the newly formed modular garment, accessory pieces may be attached to a base and/or component piece thereof.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A modular garment comprising : a base piece having a first portion of a fastening system; a component piece having a second portion of the fastening system, wherein the first and second portions of the fastening system engage to join the base piece to the component piece to form the modular garment. 2. The modular garment of claim 1, wherein the base piece comprises a flap configured to conceal the fastening system. 3. The modular garment of claim 1, wherein the base piece includes a hem and the first portion of the fastening system is positioned along the hem, and wherein the component piece includes a top portion and the second portion of the fastening system is positioned within the top portion. 4. The modular garment of claim 3, wherein the hem of the base piece comprises an angled construction, and the top portion of the component piece comprises a corresponding angled construction, and wherein the angled constructions of the hem and top portion facilitate a proper shape, fit, and/or draping of the modular garment, including by reducing any bulking of fabric at a waist or a hip of a wearer of the modular garment. 5. The modular garment of claim 4, wherein the angled construction of the base piece is approximately twenty-five degrees from horizontal. 6. The modular garment of claim 4, wherein the angled construction of the component piece is approximately twenty-five degrees from horizonal. 7. The modular garment of claim 1, wherein the base piece is a top garment. 8. The modular garment of claim 7, wherein the top garment comprises a shirt, blouse, tank top, or t-shirt. 9. The modular garment of claim 1, wherein the component piece is a bottom garment. 10. The modular garment of claim 9, wherein the bottom garment comprises pants, capris, shorts, leggings, or a skirt. 11. The modular garment of claim 1, further comprising one or more accessory pieces, wherein each of the one or more accessory pieces is attached to the base piece and/or the component piece via a fastener. 12. The modular garment of claim 11, wherein each of the one or more accessory pieces comprises a sleeve, skirt hem, stole, belt, collar, or cuff. 13. The modular garment of claim 11, wherein the fastener comprises a zipper, snap, button, tape, hook-and-loop fastener, lace, VELCROÂ®, adhesive, or magnet. 14. The modular garment of claim 1, wherein the fastening system is constructed to prevent the base piece from disengaging from the component piece. 15. The modular garment of claim 1, wherein the fastening system is a zipper. 16. A method of constructing a modular garment, comprising: providing base piece having a first portion of a fastening system; providing a component piece having a second portion of the fastening system; and engaging the first and second portions of the fastening system to join the base piece to the component piece to form the modular garment.
